LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER W. HARKONEN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS OFF CALENDAR, DUPLICATE ENTRY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-13-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE RECOGNIZED THE NAME OF DR. STEVEN GOODMAN WHOSE NAME APPEARS ON THE PLEADING. SHE HAVE GONE TO SCHOOL WITH HIM IN COLLEGE BUT NOT GOOD FRIENDS. THIS FACT DOES NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. PETITIONER W. HARKONEN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1094.5 (E),(F), THE ALJ ABUSED HER DISCRETION BY IMPROPERLY EXCLUDING ALL OF PETITIONER'S EVIDENCE AS TO BOTH LIABILITY AND PENALTY. PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1094.5 (B) (C), BECAUSE THE ALJ EXCLUDED EVIDENCE, THE ORDER REVOKING THE PETITIONER'S LICENSE WAS ARBITRARY. THE MATTER IS REMANDED TO THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA FOR RECONSIDERATION OF BOTH LIABILITY AND PENALTY PURSUANT TO CCP 1094.5(E) IN LIGHT OF THE IMPROPER EXCLUSION OF PETITIONER'S EVIDENCE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus; Ntc Of Lodging
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DENNIS RIORDAN OF RIORDAN & MORGAN, TEL. NO. 431-3472 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. BRENDA P. REYES, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5541 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed that she recognized the name of Dr. Steven Goodman whose name appears on the pleading. She have gone to school with him in College but not good friends. This fact does not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. PETITIONER W. HARKONEN's Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is Granted. Pursuant to CCP Section 1094.5 (e),(f), the ALJ abused her discretion by improperly excluding all of Petitioner's evidence as to both liability and penalty. Pursuant to CCP section 1094.5 (b) (c), because the ALJ excluded evidence, the Order revoking the Petitioner's license was arbitrary. The matter is remanded to the Medical Board of California for reconsideration of both liability and penalty pursuant to CCP 1094.5(e) in light of the improper exclusion of Petitioner's evidence. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185
